about.title: About me
about.content:
I'm a full-stack developer building practical software across frontend and backend. I work on product features, internal tools, integrations, and workflow-heavy systems in TypeScript, Angular, React, .NET-based environments, and X++.

I like finding work that can be simplified, automated, or made easier to maintain. That can mean reducing manual follow-up, improving visibility, removing duplication, or cleaning up awkward parts of a system.

What I care about most is helping people save time with software that is clearer, more reliable, and easier to work with.

cv.title: CV
cv.editWarning: But be careful. This is possible just to tweak something (or transform with AI) locally on your machine before sending it to potential employers. The changes won't be saved in any way.

hero.subtitle: I build product features, internal tools, and workflow software across frontend and backend.

fullStack.content: I work across frontend and backend, building interfaces, endpoints, integrations, and internal tools in real production systems. Most of my work is in TypeScript, .NET, and X++.

aiEnhanced.content: I use AI tools as part of my development workflow for research, implementation, and iteration, but I still care about clean code, correctness, and maintainability.

problemSolver.content: I like figuring out the real bottleneck in a system, whether that is performance, missing visibility, awkward workflows, or a feature that is harder to use than it should be.

userFocused.content: My marketing background still helps. I think about who will use the software, what they are trying to get done, and how to reduce friction instead of adding more process.

proof.heading: I work best where product work, internal tooling, and operational reality meet.

items.title: Frontend and backend in live systems
items.description: I work across interfaces, endpoints, integrations, and business logic in systems people rely on every day.

items.title: Internal tools that reduce friction
items.description: I build dashboards, task visibility tools, and small workflow software that make repeated work easier to handle.

items.title: Practical engineering over fluff
items.description: I like finding the real bottleneck, making the system clearer, and shipping something people will actually use.

contact.title: Let's get in touch
contact.subtitle: Ready to work together? I'd love to hear from you.

chatbot.placeholder: Ask about my experience and skills...

tools.title: Free Tools
tools.description: Free browser-based tools. No signup, no limits, 100% private.
tools.emailSignatureDesc: Create professional email signatures with custom colors, fonts, and social links. Includes AI-powered background removal for profile pictures.
tools.backgroundRemovalDesc: Remove backgrounds from images using AI. Works 100% in your browser - no uploads, completely private and free.

emailGenerator.title: Email Signature Generator
emailGenerator.dropImage: Drop profile picture here or click to upload
emailGenerator.previewDescription: This is how your signature will appear in emails
emailGenerator.sampleEmailBody: This is a sample email to show how your signature looks.
emailGenerator.backgroundRemovalError: Failed to remove background. Please try again.
emailGenerator.backgroundRemovalInfo: Background removal is 100% free and runs in your browser. The AI model downloads once (~50MB) and is cached for future use.

industries.overviewTitle: Focused pages for the industries I am actively reaching out to
industries.overviewDescription: These pages are designed to support outreach with concrete examples of the kinds of internal tools, workflow fixes, and small software projects that are often useful in specific industries.
industries.fitLabel: Why this can be a good fit
industries.pagePurposeBody: This is not a polished agency-style funnel. It is a practical page to show the kinds of operational problems I can help with, and the kind of narrow first project that usually makes sense.
industries.pagePurposeBodyFollowup: If that is relevant, the next useful step is usually a short conversation about one recurring workflow that is costing time today.
industries.examplesLabel: Examples of a useful first project
industries.finalCtaTitle: One recurring bottleneck is enough to start.
industries.finalCtaBody: The best next step is usually a short conversation about one real workflow that wastes time today, not a big transformation plan.
